2020: Baseline year established for coastal monitoring. Initial seagrass coverage: 420.1 km².
2021: During 2021, seagrass coverage increased by 9.9 km² compared to 2020. Sand shoals contracted by 1.0 km². Overall, the coastal ecosystem shows positive trends in biodiversity indicators.
2022: During 2022, seagrass coverage increased by 10.0 km² compared to 2021. Sand shoals contracted by 0.2 km². Overall, the coastal ecosystem shows positive trends in biodiversity indicators.
2023: During 2023, seagrass coverage increased by 10.2 km² compared to 2022. Sand shoals contracted by 0.3 km². Overall, the coastal ecosystem shows positive trends in biodiversity indicators.
2024: During 2024, seagrass coverage increased by 4.8 km² compared to 2023. Sand shoals contracted by 0.5 km². Overall, the coastal ecosystem shows positive trends in biodiversity indicators.
2025: During 2025, seagrass coverage increased by 5.0 km² compared to 2024. Sand shoals contracted by 0.5 km². Overall, the coastal ecosystem shows positive trends in biodiversity indicators.
